evidence.scientific_clarity: Sankey clearly traces 1120 raw samples through ingestion→cleaning→filtering→split stages; flow widths are proportional and column labels aid readability.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' both set; font stack is Arial/Helvetica first (no DejaVu Sans default); node labels 8pt bold, annotations 7.5pt — consistent hierarchy.
evidence.palette: Reject node uses red (#C0392B) which is directional/semantic — appropriate; but three output nodes (Train/Val/Test) share very similar greens (#117A65, #1E8449, #27AE60), making them hard to distinguish for deuteranopes; not fully colorblind-safe.
evidence.layout_density: Single-panel 9×5in figure; nodes vertically centred with small gaps; flow ribbons fill the space well; value annotations placed outside nodes without crowding; no wasted whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes what is shown, cites data.json as source, no legend needed (ribbon colour matches source node per caption); column stage labels (Ingestion/Cleaning/Filtering/Split) match described pipeline.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF produced by Matplotlib pdf backend; pdffonts shows Arial TrueType fonts embedded (CID, Identity-H encoding) — text is selectable; script explicitly sets pdf.fonttype=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (61 kB), figure.png (153 kB), figure.svg (80 kB), caption.tex (553 B), and gen_figure.py (6.9 kB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption provides specific numerical claim (600 samples = 54% to training), explains ribbon width encoding, mentions colour convention, and states data provenance — strong quantitative narrative.

overall_strengths: Fully editable vector PDF with embedded Arial fonts (pdf.fonttype=42) and matching SVG export; all required output files present.
Caption includes a concrete numerical finding (54% train split, 180 rejections), matches the figure exactly, and explains visual encoding conventions.
overall_weaknesses: Train/Val/Test nodes share three nearly-identical greens — poorly distinguishable for red-green colorblind viewers and even challenging in greyscale; a more distinct palette (e.g. blue/orange/purple) would improve accessibility.

No legend or annotation is provided for the rejection flows — the reader must infer the two rejection stages (120 at ingestion, 60 after cleaning) solely from node height rather than explicit labels on the reject ribbons.
